title: Generative Models for HVAC Fault Detection and Diagnosis in Indoor and Built Environment
abstract: Recent advances in generative models offer promising solutions for data augmentation in HVAC FDD scenarios. Common generative techniques include SMOTE, VAE, GAN, diffusion model (DM), and their hybrid approaches.

title: C-GAN-VAE: Causal Generative Adversarial Variational Autoencoder for few shot fine grained cross domain fault diagnosis for planetary gearbox
abstract: Presents C-GAN-VAE framework addressing few-shot fine-grained fault diagnosis through causal disentanglement and generative augmentation. Introduces Causal Q Network (CQN) for disentangling latent variables.

title: A fault diagnosis data augmentation method integrating multimodal non-Gaussian denoising diffusion generative adversarial network
abstract: Proposes multimodal non-Gaussian denoising diffusion GAN for data augmentation. Uses large-step noise addition in forward process and conditional GAN for parameterized denoising in reverse process.

title: Mixed-attention variational autoencoding generative adversarial networks for rolling bearing fault diagnosis
abstract: Combines VAE with GAN using mixed-attention mechanism for rolling bearing fault diagnosis. Addresses sample imbalance problems through adversarial mechanism.

title: Fault detection of high-speed train wheelset bearings based on improved auxiliary classifier generative adversarial networks and VAE
abstract: Fuses Auxiliary Classifier GAN and VAE for wheelset bearing fault detection. VAE encodes real data and uses hidden variables instead of Gaussian noise as generator inputs.

title: Deep Generative Models in Condition and Structural Health Monitoring: Opportunities, Limitations and Future Outlook
abstract: Comprehensive review of deep generative models in condition monitoring. Diffusion models mainly used for signal denoising and data augmentation. LLMs applied to fault diagnosis based on pre-trained models.

title: Fault diagnosis of gear under imbalanced sample condition using improved GAN combined with vision transformer
abstract: Applies least squares loss function to enhance GAN's ability to learn distribution characteristics. Vision transformer extracts global fault features.

title: Augmentation framework for HVAC fault diagnosis based on denoising diffusion models
abstract: Novel data augmentation framework based on Denoising Diffusion Probabilistic Models, integrating diffusion models with Gramian Angular Field transformation. Achieves 3.78% improvement over CVAE-GAN.

title: CWMS-GAN: A small-sample bearing fault diagnosis method based on continuous wavelet transform and multi-size kernel attention mechanism
abstract: Proposes continuous wavelet convolution strategy (CWCL) to reconfigure conventional convolutional layers of GAN. Enables model to capture detailed information in both time and frequency domains.

title: TFDL-GAN for Fault Diagnosis of Aero-Engine Accessory Gearbox with Imbalanced Data
abstract: TFDL-GAN significantly enhances fidelity of generated fault data under severely imbalanced conditions. Outperforms standard GAN, VAE, and SMOTE.

title: Intelligent fault diagnosis of rotating machinery based on improved hybrid dilated convolution network for unbalanced samples
abstract: Improved hybrid dilated convolution network for fault diagnosis under unbalanced sample conditions. Deep learning-based approach for rotating machinery.

title: A hybrid approach combining deep learning and signal processing for bearing fault diagnosis under imbalanced samples and multiple operating conditions
abstract: Hybrid approach based on GANs, transfer learning, wavelet transform time-frequency representations, asymmetric convolutional networks, and multi-head attention mechanism (MAC-MHA).

title: Deep Learning Techniques for Fault Diagnosis in Interconnected Systems: A Comprehensive Review and Future Directions
abstract: Comprehensive review of deep learning methods for fault diagnosis. Transfer learning and data augmentation significantly enhance performance even with limited labeled data.

title: Power Transformer Winding Fault Diagnosis Method Based on Time–Frequency Diffusion Model and ConvNeXt-1D
abstract: Data augmentation performed on original signals using time–frequency diffusion model. Combined with ConvNeXt-1D for transformer winding fault diagnosis.

title: A New Data Augmentation Model for Fault Diagnosis of Transformer Windings Under Scarce Fault Data
abstract: CDFF-TW employed to generate FRA fault data, combined with original data to augment dataset and mitigate overfitting. Investigates optimal ratio of original to generated data.

title: From limited data to reliable diagnosis: an interpretable deep learning framework for transformer fault analysis
abstract: GAN-based data augmentation generates realistic DGA samples, reducing dataset sparsity and imbalance. CNN-BiLSTM-Attention model optimized using GOOSE algorithm.

title: A Comprehensive Review of Deep Transfer Learning for Intelligent Fault Diagnosis in Rotating Machines
abstract: Reviews recent advances in deep transfer learning for intelligent fault diagnosis. Categorizes methods into four key types and evaluates their strengths.

title: From theory to industry: A survey of deep learning-enabled bearing fault diagnosis in complex environments
abstract: Systematically reviews deep learning-based research via 'data-model-application-challenges-prospects' framework. Focuses on bearing fault diagnosis in complex environments.

title: Industrial Anomaly Detection Based on Improved Diffusion Model: A Review
abstract: Diffusion models as highly effective generative models have been extensively applied to industrial anomaly detection tasks. Comprehensive review of improved diffusion models.

title: Bearing fault diagnosis based on efficient cross space multiscale CNN transformer parallelism
abstract: Efficient cross space multiscale CNN transformer parallelism for bearing fault diagnosis. Combines CNN and transformer architectures.

title: Efficient fault diagnosis in rolling bearings lightweight hybrid model
abstract: Time-Series Lightweight Transformer (TSL-Transformer) model for efficient feature extraction. Introduces multi-head attention mechanism and feedforward neural network.

title: Domain-collaborative multimodal transformer for fault diagnosis of rotating machines under noisy environments
abstract: Dual-branch architecture integrates convolutional encoders, bidirectional LSTM modules, and transformer layers to extract hierarchical features from each domain.

title: Few-shot cross-domain fault diagnosis via adversarial meta-learning
abstract: Meta-learning framework applied across source and target domains to address limited samples. Adversarial learning for domain adaptation reduces pseudo-label noise.

title: A bearing fault diagnosis method for hydrodynamic transmissions integrating few-shot learning and transfer learning
abstract: FSL + TL method outperforms conventional approaches under cold-start and imbalanced fault sample conditions. Achieves 88.75% accuracy with attention mechanism.

title: UniFault: A Fault Diagnosis Foundation Model from Bearing Data
abstract: General-purpose foundation model pretrained on over 9 billion points. Strong few-shot learning capability for real-world FD applications with limited labeled data.

title: Fault diagnosis for pump equipment based on transfer learning: the domain generalization method
abstract: FOCAL domain alignment function for transfer strategy. TFAM attention module and backbone network for cross-domain fault diagnosis. Demonstrates wide adaptability in complex industrial environments.

title: Discriminative fault diagnosis transfer learning network under joint mechanism
abstract: Novel network integrating transfer learning in rotating machinery fault diagnosis. Enhances domain adaptation by improving knowledge transfer across domains.

title: Attention-Enhanced Conditional Wasserstein GAN with Wavelet–ResNet for Fault Diagnosis Under Imbalanced Data
abstract: ACW-GAN generates high-quality synthetic data for limited fault samples. Wavelet transform employed for fault diagnosis model. FFT applied to evaluate generated sample quality.

title: Large Language and Foundation Models for Machinery Health Monitoring: A Systematic Review
abstract: Achieves ~98% fault diagnosis accuracy using merely 1.2% labeled samples. Integrating RAG and knowledge graphs mitigates hallucinations. GenAI tackles data scarcity via synthetic data generation.

title: Learning to hear broken motors: Signature-guided data augmentation for induction motor diagnostics
abstract: SGDA is first augmentation-based approach capable of learning fault patterns entirely from non-faulty operation without simulation. Fully unsupervised method.
